title: Proofpoint Outbound Email Volume Anomaly Detection
dataSources: `sourcetype=proofpoint:mta` outbound message accept / relay events with direction or sender metadata
spl:
index=email sourcetype="proofpoint:mta" earliest=-30d@d
| eval dir=coalesce(direction, Direction, msg_direction, "")
| eval from_domain=replace(coalesce(sender, Sender, mailfrom, ""), "^[^@]+@", "")
| where match(lower(dir), "(?i)out|originating") OR match(lower(_raw), "(?i)outbound")
| bin _time span=1d
| stats count as msgs by _time from_domain
| eventstats avg(msgs) as baseline stdev(msgs) as sd by from_domain
| eval sd_safe=if(sd=0 OR isnull(sd),1,sd)
| eval z=round((msgs-baseline)/sd_safe,2)
| where z>4 AND msgs>100
| sort -z
description: Sudden spikes in outbound volume from a domain or tenant slice can indicate a compromised account blasting spam or exfiltrating data via email. Baselines per sending domain highlight outliers before blocklisting harms reputation.
value: Protects mail reputation and catches bulk phishing or BEC exfiltration early by watching Proofpoint MTA throughput rather than only content verdicts.
implementation: (1) Normalize outbound detection using direction fields or message type. (2) Exclude marketing campaign domains via lookup. (3) Use 30-day baselines with z-score or MLTK if licensed. (4) On alert, pivot to `proofpoint_tap_siem` for malicious classifications. (5) Automate mailbox disables via SOAR with approval.
visualization: Line chart (daily msgs by domain), table (domain, z-score, raw count), single-value alert panel.

references.title: Splunk Docs — anomaly detection with stats

grandmaExplanation: We compare normal outbound email volume to today’s traffic so a hijacked mailbox blasting junk is noticed quickly.
